statements.content: Fullscreen modal is another option of modal dialog that covers the user viewport.
Last updated: Mar 17, 2025

> When the modal becomes too long
> When you have to go through several steps to perform the function

Previously, if there was too much content in the pop-up dialog, it was replaced with a depth-in screen. In addition, a chevron (>) icon was displayed on the function button so that the user could predict screen movement. But in general, depth-in UI structure is used to view data with hierarchies, but functions such as “package registration” or “new cell creation” do not have a hierarchy and are not suitable for using depth-in UI.

Therefore when there is a lot of data to display, configure it as a full-screen modal dialog, divide the dialog area into header, body and footer, and apply scroll only to the body area.

Since May 2022, the improvement work of UI flow that is not suitable for the depth-in UI structure has been underway. We are planning to apply a fullscreen modal dialog to all products by the end of 2023.
General use of full-screen modal:
Hierarchical information structure

1. Header
2. Body
3. Footer
4. Elevation
5. Overlay
A full-screen dialog generally consists of following elements in delivering information. (Same as basic dialog)

> Scrolling long content
> Minimum resolution

When modals become too long for user’s viewport or device, they scroll independent of the page itself.
The CTA button area must be fixed at the bottom of the viewport.
When modals become too long for user’s viewport or device, they scroll independent of the page itself.
The CTA button area must be fixed at the bottom of the viewport.

If browser size becomes smaller than minimum resolution(1440x700), display a browser scroll. If browser size is larger than minimum resolution, the footer(CTA button area) must be fixed at the bottom of the viewport.
The body of the full screen modal may be configured in various layouts.

The full screen modal popup covers the entire area except for 112 pixels at the top of the screen. The 112 pixels allow users to view app name and the title of top toolbar. The header height is 8 pixels larger than a general dialog.
network/samsung4g-access-enb/saved-…

Full-screen modal follows the structure of the general dialog. Therefore, the CTA button is also placed at the bottom. If there is a stepper, place the page change button on the footer according to the pop-up UI structure.

Always apply shadows to dialogs. Shadows follow these rules:
Shadow 1: #000000, opacity 14%, Y+24, blur 38, spread 3
Shadow 2: #000000, opacity 12%, Y+9, blur 46, spread 8
Shadow 3: #000000, opacity 20%, Y+11, blur 15, spread -7
Always apply shadows to dialogs. Shadows follow these rules:
#000000, opacity 14%, Y+24, blur 38, spread 3
#000000, opacity 12%, Y+9, blur 46, spread 8
#000000, opacity 20%, Y+11, blur 15, spread -7

The modal size adapt to screen sizes and devices. When a window is resized, the flexible area of the modal grows and shrinks.
Even if the content is less than the modal size, the full-screen modal doesn’t shrink.
• This pattern has been added to the UX 3.5 guideline.
• A guide has been added for resolution-specific resizing of full-screen modal.

• Insert horizontal lines (under the header, above the footer) when splitting the screen with a vertical divider. (#C0C0C0)
• Header height changed from 80px to 72px to match the header heights of regular modal dialogs and full-screen modal dialogs.
